_description: 75-year-old with both wallets near-zero (family ₹15K + VV ₹20K = ₹35K total). Planned CABG (expensive procedure). Tests both WALLET_INSUFFICIENT and VAY_VANDANA_DEBIT_ORDER_AMBIGUOUS flags simultaneously.

clinical.chief_complaints: Chest pain on mild exertion (CCS class III), breathlessness, unable to walk 50 metres without stopping
clinical.history_of_present_illness: 75-year-old female with known CAD (previous stent 12 months ago, treated under PM-JAY). Now presenting with worsening symptoms — angina on minimal exertion, reduced exercise tolerance. Coronary angiography done 2 weeks ago shows triple vessel disease with all three vessels critically blocked. Cardiac surgery team advises CABG.
clinical.provisional_diagnosis: Triple vessel coronary artery disease — critical, planned CABG
clinical.planned_procedure: Coronary artery bypass grafting (CABG), 3 vessels, off-pump

examination_findings.general: Elderly female, conscious, oriented, mild dyspnoea on exertion
examination_findings.cvs: S1 S2 heard, no murmurs, raised JVP, bilateral mild pitting oedema

investigations.result_summary: Coronary angiogram: LAD 90% stenosis proximal, LCX 80% stenosis mid, RCA 95% stenosis proximal. Left main 40% — non-significant. EF 35% on LV-gram. Triple vessel disease with moderately reduced LV function.
Echo: Dilated LV, EF 35%, regional wall motion abnormalities — anterior and inferior. Mild MR. No LV thrombus.

clinical.past_medical_history: Hypertension 20 years, T2DM 15 years, CKD stage 2. Prior coronary angioplasty (stent) 12 months ago under PM-JAY.
clinical.past_surgical_history: Coronary angioplasty with stent 12 months ago
clinical.notes: Both PM-JAY wallets (family + Vay Vandana) nearly exhausted from prior claims this year. Total ₹35K remaining. CABG expected to cost significantly more. Co-payment discussion essential before proceeding.
